profile.title: The Initiated Reader
profile.summary: Your instincts are already moving beyond surface symbolism. You understand that position shapes meaning, that cards in combination create narrative, and that the useful question is rarely what a card "means" in isolation.
profile.startPoint: Begin directly with spread practice. The Minor Arcana will be your focus — you don't need the Major Arcana foundations.

spread.name: Three Card
spread.description: Past, Present, Future — the spine of any reading.

card.name: The Hierophant
card.upright: The Hierophant is the bridge between the divine and the human — a priest, a teacher, an institution. He holds keys to sacred knowledge, but that knowledge comes with doctrine. In a reading he often points to inherited belief systems: religion, culture, professional convention. The question is whether you are following tradition because it serves you, or because you have not yet questioned it.
card.reversed: Breaking from tradition, or tradition that has become oppressive. Reversed, The Hierophant invites radical questioning of received wisdom — or warns that the querent is rejecting structure they actually need.
card.relational: Next to The High Priestess: external doctrine versus inner knowing. Next to The Fool: the tension between convention and the new. Next to The Devil: belief used to control.

scenario.love: A conventional relationship path — marriage, commitment ceremonies. Or the weight of family expectation on a partnership.
scenario.work: Institutional settings, professional bodies, mentors with strict methodologies.
scenario.self: Which of your beliefs actually belong to you?

card.name: The Fool
card.upright: A new chapter is opening. The Fool carries no baggage — literally. He steps off the cliff not from ignorance but from trust. In a reading, this card asks whether you are willing to begin without knowing how it ends. It speaks to pure potential, unformed energy, the courage of not yet knowing.
card.reversed: The leap is being blocked — by fear, by overthinking, or by a reckless jump taken too soon. Reversed, The Fool asks: what are you refusing to begin, or what have you begun without any thought at all?
card.relational: Next to The World: a full cycle ending and beginning. Next to The Tower: the leap may cost more than expected. Next to The High Priestess: trust your instincts before you act.

scenario.love: Beginning something new — a relationship, a feeling, a risk taken in vulnerability.
scenario.work: A career pivot, a new venture, or a moment when jumping in is the only way forward.
scenario.self: The invitation to stop rehearsing and start living.

card.name: The Chariot
card.upright: A woman closes the jaws of a lion — not with force but with gentleness. The infinity symbol floats above her. Strength is not about domination; it is about the courage to face what frightens us and transform it through love rather than will. This card speaks to the power of patience, of sustained inner work, of meeting the wild parts of yourself without flinching.
card.reversed: Self-doubt, lack of confidence, or strength being expressed as brutality rather than grace. An inner battle being lost to fear.
card.relational: Next to The Chariot: the difference between force and real power. Next to The Devil: confronting an addiction or compulsion with compassion. Next to The Moon: facing the unconscious.

scenario.love: A relationship requiring patience and inner reserves rather than action. Or the courage to show vulnerability.
scenario.work: Sustained effort over time, not a dramatic gesture. You may not see results immediately.
scenario.self: What in you needs to be faced rather than suppressed?
